public_offer_policy.description: Read-only public sellability policy for filtering dynamic offer previews. This config does not publish offers, create appointments, or change Enrollware behavior.
public_offer_policy.notes: Initial conservative public-sellability policy. ARC, HSI, and USCG are hidden until Brian explicitly approves public dynamic offers for those families.

seed_strategy_policy.description: Read-only seed strategy policy for converting public-sellable offer possibilities into intentional schedule seeds. This config does not publish offers, create appointments, or change Enrollware behavior.
seed_strategy_policy.notes: V0 conservative stack-seed strategy. Selects a small intentional seed set from public-sellable possibilities and preserves source offer lineage.

summary.minimal_safe_adjustment: No further broadening is recommended in this step. The AHA BLS IDs now reach public sellable and seed selection; keep observing selected seed spread and real Enrollware conflicts before increasing per-date seed counts.

large_files.recommendation: Keep temporarily on this review branch for exact audit reproducibility; long-term, store summarized/truncated previews in git and move full generated previews to runtime/debug or external artifact storage.

compression_pipeline.expected: Expected high fan-out: every eligible course/start combination is still a candidate.
Expected: policy hides disabled families/course IDs, off-hours starts, and caps per course/week.
Expected: stack strategy intentionally selects a small number of daily seeds.
Expected: selected seeds have container-backed appointmentDayId/courseId URL previews.
Expected in this branch: reports only; selected seed rows were not written into schedule_future.
Expected in this branch: no deploy and no public page rewrite.
Supplemental context only: existing real August inventory already present in schedule_future.
Supplemental context only: existing real August inventory already present in public_schedule.

real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_short_description: Healthcare provider BLS with high-quality CPR, AED use, ventilations, choking response, and team communication.
real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_what_to_expect: Hands-on CPR and AED practice, ventilation skills, choking response, and scenario-based team communication aligned with current CPR/ECC guidance.

real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_short_description: Advanced cardiac life support with scenario-based team response, airway, rhythm, stroke recognition, and postarrest care.
real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_what_to_expect: Systematic assessment, rhythm and medication concepts, airway and ventilation decisions, stroke recognition, postarrest care, and high-performance team communication.

real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_short_description: Pediatric advanced life support with respiratory, shock, cardiac, and team response scenarios.
real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_what_to_expect: Pediatric assessment, respiratory support, shock and cardiac arrest priorities, and team-based response practice for infants and children.

real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_short_description: Practical first aid, CPR, and AED training with updated emergency recognition topics.
real_august_enrollware_rows_sample.mapped_what_to_expect: Hands-on practice plus practical recognition topics such as choking, opioid emergencies and naloxone access, FAST stroke recognition, seizures, asthma inhaler assistance with spacer preference, severe heat illness cooling, safe rewarming, burns, eye injuries, ticks, stings, poison ivy/oak, and pulse oximetry limitations.
